title: Sagina karakorensis
intro: Sagina subulata, commonly known as Sagina karakorensis, is a type of flowering plant belonging to the Caryophyllaceae family. This plant has been traditionally used in herbal medicine, particularly in Asia, for its health benefits, which include anti-inflammatory, antioxidant, and antiviral properties, helping to alleviate symptoms of fever, cough, and respiratory issues. The bioactive constituents of Sagina karakorensis include flavonoids, phenolic acids, and saponins, which are responsible for its medicinal properties. The most common herbal preparations made with Sagina karakorensis include teas, infusions, and extracts, which are often used in traditional medicine to treat respiratory and inflammatory conditions.
taxonomy: Sagina karakorensis is a species of flowering plant that belongs to the family Caryophyllaceae. It is a member of the genus Sagina, which consists of small, annual to perennial plants. The Caryophyllaceae family is a group of flowering plants commonly known as pink or carnation family. This family is part of the larger order Caryophyllales, which includes various succulent and flowering plants. The order Caryophyllales is a part of the class Magnoliopsida, also known as dicotyledons.
